title: Choose Europe: 319 researchers awarded over €800 million for groundbreaking research
content: The European Research Council has announced the winners of its 2025 Advanced Grants, awarding €838 million to 319 leading researchers across Europe. The grants empower senior scientists to pursue high-risk, high-reward research with the potential to deliver groundbreaking discoveries.

title: EU institutions commit to ‘Europe for Culture, Culture for Europe’
content: The EU institutions have committed to placing culture at the heart of the EU project by protecting and promoting artistic freedom, cultural diversity, inclusion and fair conditions for artists. They also pledge to support the creative sectors by encouraging funding and innovation.
snippet: The EU institutions have committed to placing culture at the heart of the EU project by protecting and promoting artistic freedom, cultural diversity, inclusion and fair conditions for artists. They

title: Time for a swim: EU bathing waters remain clean
content: According to the latest EU bathing water report, 85% of Europe’s bathing sites meet ‘excellent’ standards. Austria, Bulgaria, Cyprus and Greece have the highest share for excellent quality bathing waters. EU laws on water have played a significant role in improving bathing water quality.
snippet: According to the latest EU bathing water report, 85% of Europe’s bathing sites meet ‘excellent’ standards. Austria, Bulgaria, Cyprus and Greece have the highest share for excellent quality bathing

title: New report highlights humanitarian toll of potential El Niño occurrence
content: A new EU report sets out the potential impact of the predicted El Niño climate phenomenon later this year. It predicts unusually warm weather to spread across much of the world, including in Europe, while East Africa, parts of Asia, and large parts of South and North America will get more rainfall.

title: Child safety online: EU survey confirms link between screen time and wellbeing
content: New survey confirms that excessive screen time and social media significantly impact youth’s physical and mental health. Its publication coincides with the final meeting of the special panel on child safety online, set to deliver recommendations on how to strengthen the protection of minors online.
snippet: New survey confirms that excessive screen time and social media significantly impact youth’s physical and mental health. Its publication coincides with the final meeting of the special panel on child

title: Deal on air passenger rights to protect against travel disruption
content: The EU Parliament has secured an air passenger rules deal that maintains compensation for three-hour delays, ensures faster reimbursement, fee-free child seating and flight price transparency. Passengers can also claim compensation if their flight is cancelled less than 14 days before it departs.
snippet: The EU Parliament has secured an air passenger rules deal that maintains compensation for three-hour delays, ensures faster reimbursement, fee-free child seating and flight price transparency.

title: EU and Ukraine reach historic milestone in accession negotiations
content: The EU and Ukraine have opened accession negotiations on the first so-called 'fundamentals' cluster, covering core values and principles on which the EU is built. It also includes the rule of law, EU fundamental rights, the functioning of democratic institutions and public administration reform.
snippet: The EU and Ukraine have opened accession negotiations on the first so-called 'fundamentals' cluster, covering core values and principles on which the EU is built. It also includes the rule of law, EU

title: EU and Moldova open first accession negotiations cluster
content: The EU and the Republic of Moldova have reached a significant milestone with the opening of negotiations on the first accession negotiating cluster. This includes the most critical components of EU rules on the rule of law and fundamental rights.
snippet: The EU and the Republic of Moldova have reached a significant milestone with the opening of negotiations on the first accession negotiating cluster. This includes the most critical components of EU

title: First-ever plans to unlock potential of EU islands and coastal communities
content: The European Commission has presented its first-ever, tailored strategies dedicated to support both EU islands and coastal communities. Both strategies work in tandem, reinforcing each other to address shared pressures while tackling the unique realities of islands and coastal communities alike.
snippet: The European Commission has presented its first-ever, tailored strategies dedicated to support both EU islands and coastal communities. Both strategies work in tandem, reinforcing each other to

title: EU and Republic of Korea increase strategic cooperation at summit
content: The EU and the Republic of Korea advanced their strategic partnership even further at a summit in Brussels. They also launched their competitiveness partnership, signed a digital trade agreement, and discussed current geopolitical developments.
snippet: The EU and the Republic of Korea advanced their strategic partnership even further at a summit in Brussels. They also launched their competitiveness partnership, signed a digital trade agreement, and

title: EU platform launches to help innovators and startups seeking funding
content: A new EU platform designed to support innovators and startups across Europe has launched this week. A central entry point, the EU Innovation Platform, will help innovators seeking support to bring their ideas closer to the market by connecting them with relevant available services or funding calls.
snippet: A new EU platform designed to support innovators and startups across Europe has launched this week. A central entry point, the EU Innovation Platform, will help innovators seeking support to bring

title: Updated EU law strengthens crime victims’ rights
content: The EU law on victims’ rights has been updated to make it easier to report a crime online. Victims will also gain access to the EU-wide hotline 116 006 for information and support on their rights. Children who are victims of crime will be able to call on dedicated, child-friendly support services.
snippet: The EU law on victims’ rights has been updated to make it easier to report a crime online. Victims will also gain access to the EU-wide hotline 116 006 for information and support on their rights.

title: EU prize for Women Innovators 2026 celebrates visionary women
content: The European Prize for Women Innovators recognises women who are transforming innovation into real-world impact. This year’s winners included 2026 EIC Women Innovator Katerina Spranger (Ukraine/UK), whose company harnesses AI to enhance the safety and precision of brain aneurysm treatments.
snippet: The European Prize for Women Innovators recognises women who are transforming innovation into real-world impact. This year’s winners included 2026 EIC Women Innovator Katerina Spranger (Ukraine/UK),

title: Strengthening Europe’s tech sovereignty
content: The European Commission put forward the European technological sovereignty package, to strengthen the EU’s capacity in semiconductors, AI, cloud and open source. It will help Europe become a leader in AI, strengthen digital autonomy, and help build a more sustainable digital future.
snippet: The European Commission put forward the European technological sovereignty package, to strengthen the EU’s capacity in semiconductors, AI, cloud and open source. It will help Europe become a leader in

title: EU ready for summer wildfires
content: Wildfires are increasing in frequency and severity due to climate change. In 2025 alone, over 1 million hectares burned in the EU. This summer, the EU is deploying a record number of firefighters, aircraft and emergency experts to help protect lives, homes and nature.
snippet: Wildfires are increasing in frequency and severity due to climate change. In 2025 alone, over 1 million hectares burned in the EU. This summer, the EU is deploying a record number of firefighters,

title: New plan to phase out animal testing for chemical safety assessments
content: The European Commission will start implementing a new plan to phase out animal testing for chemical safety assessments. It lays out steps towards moving to innovative non-animal approaches and preserves safety evaluations. The plan strengthens the existing EU chemicals legislation, REACH.
snippet: The European Commission will start implementing a new plan to phase out animal testing for chemical safety assessments. It lays out steps towards moving to innovative non-animal approaches and

title: EU to improve energy efficiency of buildings
content: New rules to improve the energy performance of buildings across the EU are coming into effect. They aim to support EU countries in modernising their building stock to save energy, reduce bills, and achieve a zero-emission and fully decarbonised building stock by 2050.
snippet: New rules to improve the energy performance of buildings across the EU are coming into effect. They aim to support EU countries in modernising their building stock to save energy, reduce bills, and

title: Reforming EU humanitarian aid to maximise its effectiveness
content: Humanitarian needs are at an all-time high in 2026 with 239 million people in need of assistance. The European Commission has set out concrete solutions to maximise the effectiveness and impact of EU humanitarian aid globally. This includes making it more efficient and protecting aid workers.
snippet: Humanitarian needs are at an all-time high in 2026 with 239 million people in need of assistance. The European Commission has set out concrete solutions to maximise the effectiveness and impact of EU

title: EU-funded films win big at 79th Cannes Film Festival
content: Six EU-funded films picked up awards at the latest Cannes Film Festival. Fjörd, which received EU support for its development, was awarded the prestigious Palme d'Or. MEDIA-supported Das Geträumte Abenteuer and Notre Salut took home the Jury prize and the Best Screenplay Award respectively.
snippet: Six EU-funded films picked up awards at the latest Cannes Film Festival. Fjörd, which received EU support for its development, was awarded the prestigious Palme d'Or. MEDIA-supported Das Geträumte

title: New rules strengthen the welfare and traceability of cats and dogs
content: New EU rules establish the first harmonised EU-wide standards for the welfare, breeding, identification and traceability of cats and dogs. Their identification and registration will now be mandatory, supporting responsible breeders and owners, and closing the door on illegal operators.
snippet: New EU rules establish the first harmonised EU-wide standards for the welfare, breeding, identification and traceability of cats and dogs. Their identification and registration will now be mandatory,

title: Ten years of the GDPR: your data, your rights
content: Ten years ago, the GDPR gave Europeans real control over their personal data for the first time. From the right to access your data to the right to be forgotten, its protections apply wherever you are in the EU.
snippet: Ten years ago, the GDPR gave Europeans real control over their personal data for the first time. From the right to access your data to the right to be forgotten, its protections apply wherever you are

title: EU and Mexico sign agreements to create mutual opportunities
content: The EU and Mexico have agreed to deepen their political and economic cooperation at an EU-Mexico summit. Two agreements were signed that will strengthen political dialogue and create mutual opportunities in areas such as trade, investment and clean technologies.
snippet: The EU and Mexico have agreed to deepen their political and economic cooperation at an EU-Mexico summit. Two agreements were signed that will strengthen political dialogue and create mutual

title: Celebrating Natura 2000: the world’s largest network of protected natural areas
content: On the occasion of Natura 2000 Day, we celebrate the EU’s flagship nature network, which covers more than 27 000 nature sites in Europe. Join the celebrations by taking part in a BioBlitz and exploring a Natura 2000 site near you.
snippet: On the occasion of Natura 2000 Day, we celebrate the EU’s flagship nature network, which covers more than 27 000 nature sites in Europe. Join the celebrations by taking part in a BioBlitz and

title: Agreement reached on implementing EU-US trade deal
content: The Council of the EU and the European Parliament have agreed to eliminate tariffs on all US industrial goods and provide preferential market access for certain US agricultural and seafood products. This marks an important step in delivering on the commitments made in the EU-US Joint Statement.
snippet: The Council of the EU and the European Parliament have agreed to eliminate tariffs on all US industrial goods and provide preferential market access for certain US agricultural and seafood products.

title: New rules make short-term rentals sector more transparent
content: New EU rules aimed at increasing transparency in the short-term rentals sector are now applying across Europe. They help increase safety for tourists through the reduction of illegal listings online and local communities better manage the impact of short-term rental activity.
snippet: New EU rules aimed at increasing transparency in the short-term rentals sector are now applying across Europe. They help increase safety for tourists through the reduction of illegal listings online

title: Commission proposes €144 million to help Spain, Romania, and Cyprus recover from climate disasters
content: The European Commission has proposed to mobilise €144 million from the European Union Solidarity Fund to help Spain, Romania and Cyprus recover from devastating climate-related disasters in 2025. Spain and Cyprus suffered catastrophic wildfires last summer while severe flooding struck Romania.
snippet: The European Commission has proposed to mobilise €144 million from the European Union Solidarity Fund to help Spain, Romania and Cyprus recover from devastating climate-related disasters in 2025.
